meta.changeRequestSummary: 7 fixes: focus outline style, missing focus outlines on search/CTA, spacer gap issue, mobile hamburger visibility, mobile fixed+menu-open, mobile menu max-height overflow, mobile nav border transition

changes.title: Change focus-visible outline from dashed to dotted
changes.description:
Replace all instances of `focus-visible:outline-dashed` with `focus-visible:outline-dotted` across HeaderMainItem and HeaderMainSubItem PHP and TSX files. Currently the outline-dashed class appears on:
- HeaderMainItem.php line 29: ThemeLink className for simple links
- HeaderMainItem.php line 39: button className for submenu triggers
- HeaderMainSubItem.php line 21: ThemeLink className for sub-items
- Corresponding TSX files for editor parity
changes.scope: global find-and-replace within Header block tree

changes.title: Add focus-visible outline to search submit buttons and CTA links
changes.description: Multiple interactive elements lack focus-visible outlines. Add `focus-visible:outline-dotted focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2` to each of the following elements:

elements.element: Desktop search submit button (PHP line 90, TSX line ~532)
elements.currentClasses: bg-transparent border-0 p-0 cursor-pointer shrink-0 text-white transition-colors hover:text-accent focus-visible:text-accent

elements.element: Mobile search submit button (PHP line 163, TSX line ~635)
elements.currentClasses: bg-transparent border-0 p-0 cursor-pointer shrink-0 text-white transition-colors hover:text-accent focus-visible:text-accent

elements.element: SEP mobile button/link (PHP line 45, TSX line ~456)
SEP desktop link (PHP line 114, TSX line ~558)
Mobile CTA link button (PHP line 187, TSX line ~676)
elements.currentClasses: inline-flex items-center justify-center bg-white text-charcoal border border-white rounded-full p-4 no-underline! font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] uppercase

elements.element: Mobile hamburger toggle button (PHP line 127, TSX line ~573)
elements.currentClasses: md:hidden cursor-pointer w-7 **:w-full **:h-auto transition-colors (text-white in TSX only)

changes.title: Fix spacer div adding height via parent gap
changes.description:
The outermost wrapper `<div>` on the block uses `flex flex-col gap-2` (PHP line 39, TSX line 444). The `.header-main-spacer` is the last child of this flex container, so even when it has `h-0`, the `gap-2` (8px) still adds vertical space between the nav bar div and the spacer div.

Fix approach: Change the spacer from being a flex child affected by gap to using negative margin or removing gap contribution. The cleanest fix is to make the spacer a non-participating flex item by adding `m-0 -mt-2` (to offset the gap) OR better: move the gap only between non-spacer children using `gap-0` on the wrapper and adding the gap explicitly to the SEP mobile button element as `mb-2` / margin-bottom.

Recommended approach: Replace `gap-2` on the wrapper with `gap-0` and add explicit spacing. Specifically:
1. On the outer wrapper: change `flex flex-col gap-2` to `flex flex-col`
2. On the SEP mobile link (first child, PHP line 45): add `mb-2` to create the gap only when the SEP link is present
3. The spacer (last child) already has `h-0` and will now not be affected by any gap

Alternative simpler approach: Keep `gap-2` but add `-mt-2` to the spacer div so it cancels the gap when h-0. This is simpler but less clean.

Simplest approach: Add `hidden` by default to the spacer and have JS toggle it to `block` when activating fixed mode. The JS already sets `spacer.style.height`, so it can also toggle visibility.
changes.recommendation: Use the simplest approach: add `empty:hidden` or just `hidden` to the spacer by default. In JS, when setting `spacer.style.height = header.offsetHeight + 'px'`, also call `spacer.classList.remove('hidden'); spacer.classList.add('block')`. When resetting, `spacer.classList.remove('block'); spacer.classList.add('hidden')`. This way the spacer is invisible and takes no space (including gap) when not needed.

changes.title: Fix mobile hamburger visibility (black icon on dark background)
changes.description:
The mobile toggle button on PHP line 127 has class `md:hidden cursor-pointer w-7 **:w-full **:h-auto transition-colors text-white` in the TSX but the PHP version is missing `text-white`. The SVG icons inherit currentColor, so without text-white on a bg-charcoal parent, they render in black/default color.

Fix: Add `text-white` to the button's class attribute in HeaderMain.php line 127. The TSX already has it on the button element (line 573).
changes.note: The TSX (line 573) currently has `md:hidden cursor-pointer w-7 **:w-full **:h-auto transition-colors` WITHOUT text-white on the button element itself, but the SVG children have `className="text-white"`. The PHP version has text-white on the button class but NOT on the SVG spans. Verify both PHP and TSX have `text-white` on the button itself for consistency.

changes.title: Keep header fixed when mobile menu is open and fixedOnScrollMobile is true
changes.description:
When `fixedOnScrollMobile` is true, scrolling makes the header fixed via the `.scrolling` class. But when the mobile menu opens, the scroll position or class logic may cause the header to lose its fixed positioning.

Fix in HeaderMain.js: In the `openMenu()` function, when the menu opens on mobile, force the header to stay fixed. Add logic:
1. In `openMenu()`: if `!isDesktop() && fixedScrollMobile`, add `scrolling` and `scrolling-up` classes to header, remove `scrolling-down`, and set spacer height.
2. In `updateScrollStatus()`: if `menu-open` class is present and `!isDesktop()`, skip the translate-y hide logic (don't add `scrolling-down` class).

The key principle: when the mobile menu is expanded, the header must remain visible and fixed regardless of scroll direction.

changes.title: Constrain mobile menu max-height to viewport
changes.description:
When the mobile menu expands, it can grow beyond the viewport height, making lower items unreachable without scrolling the entire page.

Fix: On the `<nav>` collapsable element (PHP line 142, TSX line 604), replace the current `menu-open:max-h-[2000px]` with a viewport-relative max-height and add overflow scrolling to the content area.

Specific changes:
1. Change `menu-open:max-h-[2000px]` to `menu-open:max-h-[calc(100dvh-80px)]` (80px approximate header bar height, or use a CSS variable `--header-bar-height` if available)
2. Add `menu-open:overflow-y-auto` to the nav element so the menu content scrolls within the constrained height
3. The `max-h` transition still works because we're transitioning from `max-h-0` to a large-but-bounded value

Note: The exact offset (80px) should account for the logo row height. The JS already calculates header height as `--header-main-height`. Consider using `menu-open:max-h-[calc(100dvh-var(--fixed-offset,80px))]` for dynamic accuracy.

changes.title: Smooth mobile nav border transition on menu close
changes.description:
When the mobile menu closes, padding changes from `menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8` to none (via max-h-0/opacity-0), causing a visible jump in the nav border position.

Fix: Add padding to the transition property list on the nav element. Currently the transition is `max-md:transition-[max-height,opacity]`. Change to `max-md:transition-[max-height,opacity,padding]` so padding animates along with the height collapse.

Additionally, instead of only adding padding when menu-open (menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8), always keep the padding on the nav and use the max-h-0 + overflow-hidden to hide it. This way there's no abrupt padding change. The recommended approach:
1. Move `pt-4 pb-8` to always be on the nav (in the base `max-md:` classes)
2. Remove `menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8`
3. The `max-h-0 overflow-hidden` already hides the content+padding when closed
4. This eliminates the jump entirely since padding never changes

decisions.decision: Use outline-dotted instead of outline-dashed for focus-visible indicators
decisions.evidence: User explicitly requested: 'Change focus-visible outline from dashed to dotted'

decisions.decision: Apply full focus-visible set (outline-dotted, outline-2, outline-white, outline-offset-2) to all interactive elements
decisions.evidence: User specified exact classes needed for search/CTA elements

decisions.decision: Use hidden/block toggle on spacer to eliminate gap contribution
decisions.evidence: Spacer with h-0 still affected by parent flex gap-2, simplest fix is visibility toggle

decisions.decision: Add text-white to hamburger button in PHP for icon visibility
decisions.evidence: Black icon on dark bg-charcoal background is invisible

decisions.decision: Force header to remain fixed when mobile menu is open
decisions.evidence: Header loses fixed positioning when menu opens on mobile

decisions.decision: Use dvh-based max-height with overflow-y-auto for mobile menu
decisions.evidence: Mobile menu can overflow viewport, needs scroll constraint

decisions.decision: Keep padding constant on nav, rely on max-h-0 overflow-hidden to hide it
decisions.evidence: Eliminates padding jump on menu close without needing transition on padding

testPage.note: Existing test page — no new variations needed for these CSS/JS fixes. QA should verify all 7 changes on the existing page.
